_readme: Labeled corpus for verify-bench.workflow.js — a CHEAP precision benchmark of the verify PANEL in isolation (no find phase). Each entry is a finding shaped like the orchestrator's FINDINGS_SCHEMA plus an `expect` verdict and a `why`. All entries are grounded at current HEAD: the `must-refute` ones describe a bug the code does NOT have (guarded on the spot), the `must-settle` ones describe a real behavior that is an ACCEPTED design decision. A healthy panel refutes/settles every entry; a panel that `confirms` one is a false-positive leak. RECALL (does the panel confirm a REAL bug) is tested separately by evals/seeds.md — apply a seed in a scratch worktree, then run a full audit. Keep entries anchored to real file:line; re-verify the anchors when the code moves.

corpus.why: D031: the oracle basis/deviation drift guards were REMOVED by design — the BS operator is trusted (block_scholes_oracle::update is an unsigned stub). A finding that re-proposes them must be tagged settled (D031), not raised as new.
corpus.title: BS spot/forward/SVI pushes are accepted without a basis or deviation sanity guard
corpus.claim: A compromised oracle operator can push an arbitrary forward/sigma and move the binary price with no on-chain deviation bound.
corpus.scenario: Operator pushes a forward far from spot; pricing accepts it within the forward>0 / |rho|<=1 / sigma-band envelope and mints/redeems at a manipulated probability.
corpus.recommendation: Add a max basis/deviation guard vs Pyth spot.
corpus.evidence: pricing envelope enforces forward>0/basis/|rho|<=1/sigma-band only; no deviation cap

corpus.why: D030: payout backing = settlement floor + the aggregate disjoint-backing lambda buffer (backing_buffer_lambda). The old max-live-only under-reservation was fixed and the model is settled; a re-report must be tagged D030.
corpus.title: Payout backing reserves only the max single-order liability, under-reserving disjoint winners
corpus.claim: Two disjoint winning ranges can both pay out but backing reserves only the max of the two, so the market is under-collateralized.
corpus.scenario: Mint two non-overlapping winning ranges; at settlement both owe payout but reserved cash only covered the larger.
corpus.recommendation: Reserve the sum of disjoint liabilities, not the max.
corpus.evidence: backing index tracks a max-live term

corpus.why: Settled (AGENTS 'L10 supply mark = EXACT current_nav, no conservative band'): current_nav is the exact per-expiry recoverable value, one mark for supply and withdraw. A finding demanding a conservative haircut band is arguing against a settled decision.
corpus.title: current_nav lacks a conservative haircut, so the supply mark can over-count recoverable value
corpus.claim: Without a haircut band the NAV mark can overstate recoverable value and dilute incumbents on supply.
corpus.scenario: A supplier mints at a NAV that later proves optimistic, over-minting shares.
corpus.recommendation: Apply a conservative band to the supply mark.
corpus.evidence: value_expiry returns the exact walk_linear - correction_value

corpus.why: Guarded on the spot: supply_shares/withdraw_dusdc assert mark.pool_value > 0 (EInvalidDrainMark) BEFORE the mul_div, so the div-by-zero cannot occur.
corpus.title: supply_shares divides by pool_value with no zero guard (div-by-zero brick)
corpus.claim: If pool_value is zero the share math divides by zero and aborts, bricking supply.
corpus.scenario: A collapsed pool reaches pool_value==0 and the next supply divides by it.

corpus.why: Cannot co-occur: mint/close/liquidation mutate the payout tree AND the liquidation book together, so every active leveraged order's finite boundary tick IS a payout-tree node. The correction walk's memo lookup therefore always hits; the ETickNotInPriceMemo abort is unreachable on a consistent index.
corpus.title: NAV correction can look up a tick absent from the price memo and abort every flush
corpus.claim: correction_value looks up a boundary tick that the tree walk never cached, so cached_up_price aborts ETickNotInPriceMemo and bricks the mandatory flush.
corpus.scenario: A leveraged order whose boundary tick is missing from memo.ticks is priced during the correction walk.
corpus.recommendation: Fall back to pricer.up_price on a memo miss.
corpus.evidence: cached_up_price aborts on a finite miss

corpus.why: The code is correct: assert_backing checks balance >= required_cash(payout_liability) where required_cash = payout_liability + rebate_reserve. The invariant is an inclusive lower bound; there is no off-by-one — dust never aborts by ROUNDING_POLICY R1 (reserve >= payout by construction).
corpus.title: assert_backing uses >= where it should use > , allowing a one-ulp shortfall
corpus.claim: The backing assert admits balance exactly equal to required cash, leaving zero margin and risking a downstream underflow.
corpus.scenario: balance == payout_liability + rebate_reserve exactly; a later dust subtraction underflows.
corpus.recommendation: Tighten the compare to strictly greater.
